rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LALANNE VS. MICHELE ARMANINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Recall And Quash Qrit Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 8, 2014, Line 3, RESPONDENTs MICHELE ARMANINO and LALANNE LLC's Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution. The motion is granted. First, while it is not entirely clear, the Court tends to believe that respondent may have satisfied the judgment. The arbitrator's Final Award and the judgment orders respondent to make a distribution and respondent made the distribution. The award/judgment does not award a certain sum of damages. The arbitrator addressed the offset issue and refused to alter the award (RT 51:17-54:9). Second, in light of the uncertainty regarding satisfaction of the judgment, it is improper to allow the writ of execution to remain in place. Third, an appeal generally extinguishes the lien that a writ of execution creates. See Adir International, LLC v. Sup. Crt. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 996, 1001. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PALMISANO DELIVERY SERVICE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC. VS. WHEELHOUSE SOLUTIONS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 8, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTs WHEELHOUSE SOLUTIONS, LLC, FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC and CHAMPION EXPOSITION SERVICES, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. The relief sought in the Notice of Motion does not pertain to a specific duty. (CRC 3.1350(b).) "Freeman seeks summary adjudication as a matter of law with regard to PDS's claim that Freeman has or had a contractual duty to provide PDS with business pursuant to the September 2010 Exclusive Services Agreement between the plaintiff and [Champion]. Freeman seeks an order holding that Freeman had no independent obligation to PDS pursuant to the Agreement." (Notice of Motion, p. 2:1-5.) There is a triable issue of fact as to the meaning of "independent". Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PALMISANO DELIVERY SERVICE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC. VS. WHEELHOUSE SOLUTIONS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 8, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF PALMISANO DELIVERY SERVICE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. For Issue nos. 1 - 3, Plaintiff moves for summary adjudication of separate elements of the first cause of action for breach of contract that does not completely dispose of the cause action. (CCP § 437c(f)(1).) There is no indication that Plaintiff has complied with CCP § 437c(s) to adjudicate part of a cause of action. For Issue no. 4, Plaintiff moves for summary adjudication of the Answer, but fails to identify any specific affirmative defense in its Notice of Motion. (CRC 3.1350(b).) Notwithstanding this procedural issue, Freeman responded that it did not contribute directly or proximately to the incident. (Plaintiff's Evidence, Exhibit J., p. 12:1-2.) Thus, Plaintiff has not met its initial burden of proof to show that the affirmative defenses are unsupported by factually devoid discovery responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: UNION BANK, N.A. VS. FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF P & T K PROPERTIES, LLC'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Cross-complaint stricken. No other sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. JDS BUILDERS GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 8, 2014, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT LEVY DESIGN PARTNERS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. JDS Builders Group, Inc. (JDS) cannot show ultimate facts required to state a cause of action for negligent representation. The specifications were neither factual nor positive assertions. Even if the specifications were regarded as assertions of past or existing fact, such statements were not made without any reasonable ground for believing them to be untrue. (See Civ. Code, § 1710, subd. 2; Civ. Code, § 1572, subd. 2; Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc., (2003) 30 Cal. 4th 167, 174) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
